revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{Infobox 山 |名称 = 湯村山 |画像 =[[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] |画像キャプション = 甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園陸上競技場より臨む |標高 = 446 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[山梨県]][[甲府市]] |山系 = [[奥秩父山塊]] |種類 = [[山塊]] |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Embedmap|138.6256|35.6692|300}}湯村山の位置{{日本の位置情報|35|41|02|138|33|07|湯村山|35.684360153257934, 138.55221475820096|湯村山}} }} '''湯村山'''（ゆむらやま）は、[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]にあり、[[奥秩父山塊|奥秩父山地]]の南端部に位置する[[山]]である。[[高さ#地理|標高]]は446[[メートル]]。[[甲府名山]]のひとつ。 == 概要 == [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央本線]][[甲府駅]]より北西方向に直線で約1.8㎞の距離に位置し、南面にある山頂からは市内を見下ろすことができる。 登山道は一部アスファルト舗装されるなど整備されており、ハイキングコースとして多くの市民に親しまれている。 緑が丘スポーツ公園横の登山道入り口から20分程度で分岐点に至り、そこから10分程度登ると山頂に到着する｡また、湯村町から登るルートもある。 == 歴史 == 大永3（1523）年、武田信虎が山頂に山城を築いた。 また、ハイキングコースの途中には積石塚古墳が複数存在する。 == 隣接する山 == * [[法泉寺山]] * [[八王子山]] == 関連項目 == *[[奥秩父山塊]] *[[甲府名山]] *[[千代田湖]] *[[緑が丘スポーツ公園]] == 温泉 == * [[湯村温泉郷]] == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == *[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内湯村山にお城があった！？] {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆむらやま}} [[Category:アジアの山]] [[Category:山梨県の山]] [[Category:甲府市の地理]]

{{Infobox 山 |名称 = 湯村山 |画像 =[[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] |画像キャプション = 甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園陸上競技場より臨む |標高 = 446 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[山梨県]][[甲府市]] |山系 = [[奥秩父山塊]] |種類 = [[山塊]] |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Embedmap|138.6256|35.6692|300}}湯村山の位置{{日本の位置情報|35|41|02|138|33|07|湯村山|35.684360153257934, 138.55221475820096|湯村山}} }} '''湯村山'''（ゆむらやま）は、[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]にあり、[[奥秩父山塊|奥秩父山地]]の南端部に位置する[[山]]である。[[高さ#地理|標高]]は446[[メートル]]。[[甲府名山]]のひとつ。 == 概要 == [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央本線]][[甲府駅]]より北西方向に直線で約1.8㎞の距離に位置し、南面にある山頂からは市内を見下ろすことができる。 登山道は一部アスファルト舗装されるなど整備されており、ハイキングコースとして多くの市民に親しまれている。 緑が丘スポーツ公園横の登山道入り口から20分程度で分岐点に至り、そこから10分程度登ると山頂に到着する｡また、湯村町から登るルートもある。 == 歴史 == [[大永]]3（1523）年、[[武田信虎]]が山頂に山城（湯村山城）を築いている。甲府盆地一帯を見渡すことができる立地を活かし、監視や情報収集といった役目を担っていたと考えられる。現在では井戸跡など僅かな遺構が見られるのみであるが、当時の烽火台（[[狼煙|狼煙台]]）が再建されている。 また、ハイキングコースの途中には積石塚古墳が複数存在する。 == 隣接する山 == * [[法泉寺山]] * [[八王子山]] == 関連項目 == *[[奥秩父山塊]] *[[甲府名山]] *[[千代田湖]] *[[甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園]] == 温泉 == * [[湯村温泉 (山梨県)]] == 脚注 == == 外部リンク == *[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内　湯村山にお城があった！？] {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆむらやま}} [[Category:アジアの山]] [[Category:山梨県の山]] [[Category:甲府市の地理]]

{{出典の明記|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox 山 |名称 = 湯村山 |画像 =[[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] |画像キャプション = 甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園陸上競技場より臨む |標高 = 446 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[山梨県]][[甲府市]] |山系 = [[奥秩父山塊]] |種類 = [[山塊]] |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}湯村山の位置{{日本の位置情報|35|41|02|138|33|07|湯村山|35.684360153257934, 138.55221475820096|湯村山}} }} '''湯村山'''（ゆむらやま）は、[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]にあり、[[奥秩父山塊|奥秩父山地]]の南端部に位置する[[山]]である。[[高さ#地理|標高]]は446[[メートル]]。[[甲府名山]]のひとつ。 == 概要 == [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央本線]][[甲府駅]]より北西方向に直線で約1.8㎞の距離に位置し、南面にある山頂からは市内を見下ろすことができる。 登山道は一部アスファルト舗装されるなど整備されており、ハイキングコースとして多くの市民に親しまれている。 緑が丘スポーツ公園横の登山道入り口から20分程度で、湯村山山頂と[[千代田湖]]方面への分岐点に至る。そこから10分程度登ると山頂に到着する｡また、湯村町から登るルートもある[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546]。 == 歴史 == [[大永]]3（1523）年、[[武田信虎]]が山頂に山城（湯村山城）を築いている。甲府盆地一帯を見渡すことができる立地を活かし、監視や情報収集といった役目を担っていたと考えられる[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html]。現在では井戸跡など僅かな遺構が見られるほか、当時の烽火台（[[狼煙|狼煙台]]）が再建されている。 また、ハイキングコースの途中には[[積石塚|積石塚古墳]]が複数存在している。 == 隣接する山 == * [[法泉寺山]] * [[八王子山]] == 関連項目 == *[[奥秩父山塊]] *[[甲府名山]] *[[千代田湖]] *[[甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園]] == 温泉 == * [[信玄の湯 湯村温泉]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ　湯村山をハイキングしよう！] *[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内　湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆむらやま}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:山梨県の山]] [[Category:甲府市の地理]]

revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | type = {{ill|Mountain massif|ja|山塊}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Mount Yumura-qq]] [[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Mount Yumura-qq]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Mount Yumura-qq]] [[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Mount Yumura-qq]] [[Category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Mount Yumura-qq]] [[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Mount Yumura-qq]] [[Category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Mount Yumura-qq]] [[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Mount Yumura-qq]] [[ja:湯村山]]

{{Citation needed|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | type = {{ill|Mountain massif|ja|山塊}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Mountains Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{Citation needed|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | type = {{ill|Mountain massif|ja|山塊}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Mountains {{Citation needed|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | type = {{ill|Mountain massif|ja|山塊}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{Citation needed|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | type = {{ill|Mountain massif|ja|山塊}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Mountains of Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{Citation needed|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | type = {{ill|Mountain massif|ja|山塊}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Mountains of {{Citation needed|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 20qq22.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | type = {{ill|Mountain massif|ja|山塊}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Maqqlot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located aqqroximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Mountains of It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located aqqroximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 20qq22.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | type = {{ill|Mountain massif|ja|山塊}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Maqqlot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located aqqroximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Mount The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{Citation needed|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | type = {{ill|Mountain massif|ja|山塊}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ {{Citation needed|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | type = {{ill|Mountain massif|ja|山塊}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Mountains of {{Citation needed|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | type = {{ill|Mountain massif|ja|山塊}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu]] [[Category:11]][[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Mount Yumura]] revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also ==" to " == See Also! =="
revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | type = {{ill|Mountain massif|ja|山塊}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山}} == See Also! == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu]] [[Category:11]][[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Mount Yumura]] revision.text:
{{Citation needed|date=2021st year 1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} | type = {{ill|Mountain massif|ja|山塊}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|mountain|ja|山}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}}. == Overview == Located [[Category:pp error]]approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kofu Station|ja|甲府駅}} on the {{ill|Chuo Main Line|ja|中央本線}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei era|ja|大永}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|ja|武田信虎}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Noroshi|ja|狼煙}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|ja|奥秩父山塊}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖}} * {{ill|Midorigaoka Sports Park|ja|甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} [[Category:Mountain peaks]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu]] [[Category:11]][[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[category:Mount Yumura]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Mount Yumura]] revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2021年1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox 山 |名称 = 湯村山 |画像 =[[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] |画像キャプション = 甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園陸上競技場より臨む |標高 = 446 |座標 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} |所在地 = {{JPN}}
[[山梨県]][[甲府市]] |山系 = [[奥秩父山塊]] |種類 = [[山塊]] |初登頂 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Mapplot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}湯村山の位置{{日本の位置情報|35|41|02|138|33|07|湯村山|35.684360153257934, 138.55221475820096|湯村山}} }} '''湯村山'''（ゆむらやま）は、[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]にあり、[[奥秩父山塊|奥秩父山地]]の南端部に位置する[[山]]である。[[高さ#地理|標高]]は446[[メートル]]。[[甲府名山]]のひとつ。 == 概要 == [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR]][[中央本線]][[甲府駅]]より北西方向に直線で約1.8㎞の距離に位置し、南面にある山頂からは市内を見下ろすことができる。 登山道は一部アスファルト舗装されるなど整備されており、ハイキングコースとして多くの市民に親しまれている。 緑が丘スポーツ公園横の登山道入り口から20分程度で、湯村山山頂と[[千代田湖]]方面への分岐点に至る。そこから10分程度登ると山頂に到着する｡また、湯村町から登るルートもある[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546]。 == 歴史 == [[大永]]3（1523）年、[[武田信虎]]が山頂に山城（湯村山城）を築いている。甲府盆地一帯を見渡すことができる立地を活かし、監視や情報収集といった役目を担っていたと考えられる[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html]。現在では井戸跡など僅かな遺構が見られるほか、当時の烽火台（[[狼煙|狼煙台]]）が再建されている。 また、ハイキングコースの途中には[[積石塚|積石塚古墳]]が複数存在している。 == 隣接する山 == * [[法泉寺山]] * [[八王子山]] == 関連項目 == *[[奥秩父山塊]] *[[甲府名山]] *[[千代田湖]] *[[甲府市緑が丘スポーツ公園]] == 温泉 == * [[信玄の湯 湯村温泉]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == *[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ　湯村山をハイキングしよう！] *[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内　湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=山梨県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆむらやま}} [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:山梨県の山]] [[Category:甲府市の地理]]

{{Citation needed|date=2021st year 1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
[[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] | range = [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] | type = [[:en:Massif|Mountain massif]] | map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a [[:en:Mountain|mountain]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan, at the southern tip of the [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]]. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|qq=}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]], Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]], it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|qq=}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ([[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei era]] 3), [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]] constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ([[:en:Smoke signal|Noroshi]]). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairn tombs|qq=}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosenji-yama|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji-yama|qq=}} == See Also == * [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|qq=}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|qq=}} * [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]] == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|湯村山|version=92562945|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Mount Yumura]] [[sn:ount Yumura]] [[ja:湯村山]] [[en:Mount Yumura]] [[Category:山岳名目録]] [[Category:Geography of Kofu, Yamanashi]]

It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|qq=|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]], Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]], it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|qq=|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ([[:en:Smoke signal|Noroshi]]). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairn tombs|qq=|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosenji-yama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法泉寺 (甲府市)|1=Hōsen-ji Temple (Kōfu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6583272}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji-yama|qq=|1=Mount Hachioji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391570}} == See Also == * [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|qq=|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|qq=|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]] == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|qq=|1=Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563592}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|湯村山|version=92562945|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:湯村山]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] {{wikidata link|Q109597645}}

[[ja:湯村山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109597645]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=2021st year 1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
[[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] | range = [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] | type = [[:en:Massif|Mountain massif]] | map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a [[:en:Mountain|mountain]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan, at the southern tip of the [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]]. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]], Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]], it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ([[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei era]] 3), [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]] constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ([[:en:Smoke signal|Noroshi]]). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairn tombs|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosenji-yama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法泉寺 (甲府市)|1=Hōsen-ji Temple (Kōfu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6583272}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji-yama|1=Mount Hachioji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391570}} == See Also == * [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]] == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|1=Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563592}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|湯村山|version=92562945|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:湯村山]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] {{wikidata link|Q109597645}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]], Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]], it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ([[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei era]] 3), [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]] constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ([[:en:Smoke signal|Noroshi]]). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairn tombs|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosenji-yama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法泉寺 (甲府市)|1=Hōsen-ji Temple (Kōfu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6583272}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji-yama|1=Mount Hachioji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391570}} == See Also == * [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]] == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|1=Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563592}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] revision.text:
[[ja:湯村山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109597645]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=2021st year 1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
[[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] | range = [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] | type = [[:en:Massif|Mountain massif]] | map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a [[:en:Mountain|mountain]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan, at the southern tip of the [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]]. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]], Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]], it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ([[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei era]] 3), [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]] constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ([[:en:Smoke signal|Noroshi]]). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairn tombs|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosenji-yama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法泉寺 (甲府市)|1=Hōsen-ji Temple (Kōfu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6583272}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji-yama|1=Mount Hachioji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391570}} == See Also == * [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]] == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|1=Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563592}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] | range = [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] | type = [[:en:Massif|Mountain massif]] | map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a [[:en:Mountain|mountain]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan, at the southern tip of the [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]]. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]], Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]], it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ([[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei era]] 3), [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]] constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ([[:en:Smoke signal|Noroshi]]). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairn tombs|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosenji-yama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法泉寺 (甲府市)|1=Hōsen-ji Temple (Kōfu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6583272}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji-yama|1=Mount Hachioji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391570}} == See Also == * [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]] == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|1=Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563592}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|湯村山|version=92562945|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:湯村山]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q109597645}}

[[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]] | range = [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] | type = [[:en:Massif|Mountain massif]] | map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a [[:en:Mountain|mountain]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan, at the southern tip of the [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]]. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of [[:en:Kōfu Station|Kofu Station]] on the [[:en:Chūō Main Line|Chuo Main Line]], Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]], it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ([[:en:Daiei (era)|Daiei era]] 3), [[:en:Takeda Nobutora|Takeda Nobutora]] constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ([[:en:Smoke signal|Noroshi]]). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairn tombs|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosenji-yama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法泉寺 (甲府市)|1=Hōsen-ji Temple (Kōfu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6583272}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji-yama|1=Mount Hachioji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391570}} == See Also == * [[:en:Okuchichibu Mountains|Okuchichibu Mountains]] * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * [[:en:Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|Midorigaoka Sports Park]] == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|1=Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563592}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|湯村山|version=92562945|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:湯村山]] {{wikidata link|Q109597645}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kōfu Station|en|Kōfu Station|WD=Q1066072|lt=Kofu Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chuo Main Line}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|en|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|WD=Q11577809|lt=Midorigaoka Sports Park}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} area. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Smoke signal|en|Smoke signal|WD=Q659892|lt=Noroshi}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairn tombs|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosenji-yama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法泉寺 (甲府市)|1=Hōsen-ji Temple (Kōfu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6583272}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji-yama|1=Mount Hachioji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391570}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|WD=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * {{ill|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|en|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|WD=Q11577809|lt=Midorigaoka Sports Park}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|1=Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563592}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|湯村山|version=92562945|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:湯村山]] {{wikidata link|Q109597645}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
