revision.text:
[[ja:湯村山]] [[ja:湯村山]] [[ja:湯村山]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q109597645]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=2021st year 1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|WD=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains}} | type = {{ill|Massif|en|Massif|WD=Q1061151|lt=Mountain massif}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|Mountain|en|Mountain|WD=Q8502|lt=mountain}} located in {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|WD=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kōfu Station|en|Kōfu Station|WD=Q1066072|lt=Kofu Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chuo Main Line}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|en|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|WD=Q11577809|lt=Midorigaoka Sports Park}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei (era)|en|Daiei (era)|WD=Q1193564|lt=Daiei era}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|en|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628|lt=Takeda Nobutora}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Smoke signal|en|Smoke signal|WD=Q659892|lt=Noroshi}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairn tombs|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosenji-yama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法泉寺 (甲府市)|1=Hōsen-ji Temple (Kōfu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6583272}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji-yama|1=Mount Hachioji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391570}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|WD=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * {{ill|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|en|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|WD=Q11577809|lt=Midorigaoka Sports Park}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|1=Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563592}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|湯村山|version=92562945|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:湯村山]] {{wikidata link|Q109597645}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairn tombs|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosenji-yama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法泉寺 (甲府市)|1=Hōsen-ji Temple (Kōfu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6583272}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji-yama|1=Mount Hachioji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391570}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|WD=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * {{ill|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|en|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|WD=Q11577809|lt=Midorigaoka Sports Park}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|1=Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563592}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|湯村山|version=92562945|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:湯村山]] {{wikidata link|Q109597645}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
[[ja:湯村山]] [[ja:湯村山]] [[ja:湯村山]] {{Citation needed|date=2021st year 1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|WD=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains}} | type = {{ill|Massif|en|Massif|WD=Q1061151|lt=Mountain massif}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|Mountain|en|Mountain|WD=Q8502|lt=mountain}} located in {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kofu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|WD=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kōfu Station|en|Kōfu Station|WD=Q1066072|lt=Kofu Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|WD=Q1078110|lt=Chuo Main Line}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|en|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|WD=Q11577809|lt=Midorigaoka Sports Park}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei (era)|en|Daiei (era)|WD=Q1193564|lt=Daiei era}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|en|Takeda Nobutora|WD=Q1192628|lt=Takeda Nobutora}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Smoke signal|en|Smoke signal|WD=Q659892|lt=Noroshi}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairn tombs|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosenji-yama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法泉寺 (甲府市)|1=Hōsen-ji Temple (Kōfu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6583272}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji-yama|1=Mount Hachioji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391570}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|WD=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * {{ill|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|en|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|WD=Q11577809|lt=Midorigaoka Sports Park}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|1=Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563592}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|湯村山|version=92562945|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:湯村山]] {{wikidata link|Q109597645}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairn tombs|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597152}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosenji-yama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法泉寺 (甲府市)|1=Hōsen-ji Temple (Kōfu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6583272}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji-yama|1=Mount Hachioji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11391570}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|WD=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains|1=Okuchichibu Mountains}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11405318}} * {{ill|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|en|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|WD=Q11577809|lt=Midorigaoka Sports Park|1=Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|1=Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563592}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|湯村山|version=92562945|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:湯村山]] {{wikidata link|Q109597645}} [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[ja:湯村山]] [[ja:湯村山]] [[ja:湯村山]] {{Citation needed|date=2021st year 1月26日 (火) 04:17 (UTC)}} {{Infobox mountain | name = Yumura-yama | photo = [[File:Mt.Yumura 201122.jpg|300px|]] | photo_caption = Viewed from the Kofu City Midorigaoka Sports Park Athletic Stadium | elevation_m = 446 | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|02|N|138|33|07|E|type:mountain_region:JP-19|display=inline,title}} | location = {{JPN}}
{{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|qid=Q201932|lt=Kofu|1=Kōfu}} | range = {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|qid=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains|1=Okuchichibu Mountains}} | type = {{ill|Massif|en|Massif|qid=Q1061151|lt=Mountain massif|1=Massif}} | map = {{Location map|Japan Ma11lot|coordinates={{Coord|35.6692|138.6256}}|caption=|width=300}}Location of Yumura-yama }} '''Yumura-yama''' (湯村山) is a {{ill|Mountain|en|Mountain|qid=Q8502|lt=mountain|1=Mountain}} located in {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|qid=Q201932|lt=Kofu|1=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|qid=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}, Japan, at the southern tip of the {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|qid=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains|1=Okuchichibu Mountains}}. It has an elevation of 446 meters and is one of the {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109365123}}. == Overview == Located approximately 1.8 km northwest of {{ill|Kōfu Station|en|Kōfu Station|qid=Q1066072|lt=Kofu Station|1=Kōfu Station}} on the {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|qid=Q1078110|lt=Chuo Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}}, Yumura-yama offers a panoramic view of Kofu City from its summit. The hiking trails are well-maintained, with sections paved with asphalt, making them popular among local residents. From the trailhead near {{ill|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|en|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|qid=Q11577809|lt=Midorigaoka Sports Park|1=Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium}}, it takes about 20 minutes to reach the junction leading to the Yumura-yama summit and the {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11405318}} area. The summit is a further 10 minutes from the junction. Another route starts from Yumura Town.[https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] == History == In 1523 ({{ill|Daiei (era)|en|Daiei (era)|qid=Q1193564|lt=Daiei era|1=Daiei (era)}} 3), {{ill|Takeda Nobutora|en|Takeda Nobutora|qid=Q1192628|lt=Takeda Nobutora|1=Takeda Nobutora}} constructed a mountain fortress (Yumura-yama Castle) at the summit. Its location allowed for extensive surveillance and intelligence gathering over the Kofu Basin. Today, remnants such as a well and some minor ruins remain, alongside a reconstructed beacon fire platform ({{ill|Smoke signal|en|Smoke signal|qid=Q659892|lt=Noroshi|1=Smoke signal}}). The hiking trail also features several {{ill|Cairn tombs|ja|積石塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cairn tombs|1=stone mound tomb|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11597152}} along the way.[https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] == Neighboring Mountains == * {{ill|Hosenji-yama|ja|法泉寺山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hosenji-yama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 法泉寺 (甲府市)|1=Hōsen-ji Temple (Kōfu)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6583272}} * {{ill|Hachioji-yama|ja|八王子山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachioji-yama|1=Mount Hachioji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11391570}} == See Also == * {{ill|Okuchichibu Mountains|en|Okuchichibu Mountains|qid=Q1738280|lt=Okuchichibu Mountains|1=Okuchichibu Mountains}} * {{ill|Kofu Meizan|ja|甲府名山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Meizan|1=Kofu Meizan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109365123}} * {{ill|Chiyoda Lake|ja|千代田湖|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiyoda Lake|1=Lake Chiyoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11405318}} * {{ill|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|en|Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium|qid=Q11577809|lt=Midorigaoka Sports Park|1=Kofu Midorigaoka Sports Park Stadium}} == Onsen == * {{ill|Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|ja|信玄の湯 湯村温泉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|1=Shingen-no-Yu Yumura Onsen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11563592}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://kofu-tourism.com/course/546 甲府観光ナビ 湯村山をハイキングしよう！] * [https://www.city.kofu.yamanashi.jp/senior/kamejii/064.html 甲府市公式サイト内 湯村山にお城があった！？] {{Mountain-stub|pref=Yamanashi Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|湯村山|version=92562945|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:湯村山]] {{wikidata link|Q109597645}} [[Category:Mountain Gazetteers]] [[Category:Mountains of Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
